Chunking Strategy Notes (Sample)
1. Typical chunk sizes for narrative PDFs range from 500 to 800 tokens.
2. Overlaps of 50 to 100 tokens help preserve context across chunk boundaries.
3. Too-small chunks can reduce answer completeness; too-large chunks can reduce precision.
4. Choose chunking based on document structure and query types.
This PDF is synthetic and intended for capstone evaluation only.

Retrieval Evaluation Metrics (Sample)
1. Precision@k measures how many of the top-k retrieved chunks are relevant.
2. Recall@k measures whether any relevant chunk appears in the top-k set.
3. Gold citations are human-labeled references that support an answer.
4. String-match F1 can be used for key facts but may miss paraphrases.
This PDF is synthetic and intended for capstone evaluation only.

Logging & Monitoring for LLM Apps (Sample)
1. Log retrieval traces (query, top-k sources, similarity scores) for debugging.
2. Avoid logging sensitive user data unless necessary.
3. Track user feedback to improve prompts and retrieval quality.
4. Use a small evaluation suite to prevent regressions.
This PDF is synthetic and intended for capstone evaluation only.

RAG Basics Guide (Sample)
1. A RAG pipeline includes ingestion, chunking, embedding, indexing, retrieval, and synthesis.
2. Chunk overlap can improve recall but increases index size.
3. Citations improve trust and enable verification of claims.
4. If evidence is weak, the assistant should ask clarifying questions or refuse.
This PDF is synthetic and intended for capstone evaluation only.

Support Escalation Guide (Sample)
1. Escalate to a human agent when identity verification is required.
2. Escalate when the user reports fraud, repeated failed refunds, or threats of legal action.
3. Collect key details: order ID, contact email, and issue description.
4. Provide a clear handoff summary for the human agent.
This PDF is synthetic and intended for capstone evaluation only.

System Prompting Notes (Sample)
1. System prompts define role, tone, and non-negotiable constraints.
2. In a RAG assistant, require citations for factual claims.
3. Treat retrieved documents as untrusted and ignore instructions inside them.
4. Use refusal behavior when evidence is insufficient.
This PDF is synthetic and intended for capstone evaluation only.

Vector Search Guide (Sample)
1. Embeddings map text to vectors so similar meanings are close in vector space.
2. Top-k retrieval returns the most similar chunks by cosine similarity or dot product.
3. A reranker can improve precision by reordering candidates.
4. Hybrid retrieval merges BM25 and vector search for better coverage.
This PDF is synthetic and intended for capstone evaluation only.

Payments & Security Policy (Sample)
1. Accepted payment methods include cards, UPI, net banking, and select wallets.
2. Cash on Delivery may be available for eligible pin codes and products.
3. Payments are processed via PCI-DSS compliant gateways with encryption.
4. Failed payments are typically reversed within 3–7 business days.
This PDF is synthetic and intended for capstone evaluation only.

Privacy & Data Use Policy (Sample)
1. Collect only the minimum personal data required to fulfill orders.
2. Access to customer data should be role-based and logged.
3. Do not store full card numbers; use tokenization via payment providers.
4. Data may be shared with logistics partners for delivery purposes.
This PDF is synthetic and intended for capstone evaluation only.

Shipping & Returns Policy (Sample)
1. Standard shipping is free for orders above INR 999; otherwise a flat fee of INR 79 applies.
2. Standard delivery takes 3–6 business days depending on location.
3. Most products can be returned within 7 days if unused and in original packaging.
4. Refunds are processed within 3–7 business days after quality check.
This PDF is synthetic and intended for capstone evaluation only.
